name: _MEMORY_WRITE
instruction: Provide new data to write to <memory>

params.new_memory: DICTIONARY Of 'NEW KEYS AND VALUES TO BE ADDED OR UPDATED'

name: _ADD_SCHEDULE
instruction: Add a scheduled reminder or task. CRITICAL: If user says 'schedule this prompt' or 'schedule to do this' or 'run this prompt' → type MUST be 'prompt' (agent runs instruction at scheduled time), NOT 'reminder'. limit_channel: [] = all channels. Relative times (e.g. 'in 3 mins', 'in 1 hour'): set relative_minutes to the number, leave datetime empty. Absolute times (e.g. 'tomorrow at 5pm'): set datetime as YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM. datetime must be YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM only—no natural language. type 'reminder': data:{message:'...'} only, omit action/param. type 'action': data:{message:'...', action:'...', param:{...}}. type 'prompt': user wants to RUN a free-form instruction - set data:{message:'instruction text'}. CRITICAL: data must be an object. Use data:{message:'...'} never data.message as top-level key. For prompt: extract instruction after colon into data object. Example: data:{message:'get current datetime and send it to telegram'}. Example action: data:{message:'Update Task to Done',action:'_MEMORY_WRITE',param:{new_memory:{Task:Done}}}. Example broadcast: data:{message:'Hello',action:'_BROADCAST_MSG',param:{message:'Hello',channels:[]}}.

params.datetime: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM for absolute times. Leave empty when using relative_minutes
params.relative_minutes: number e.g. 3 for 'in 3 mins'. Required when user says 'in X minutes'

data.action: string (only when type is action, e.g. _MEMORY_WRITE, _BROADCAST_MSG)
data.param: object (only when type is action, params for that action)

name: _DELETE_SCHEDULE
instruction: Remove scheduled reminder(s). Use when user wants to cancel, delete, or remove a reminder. Match by datetime and/or message (message is substring match, case-insensitive). At least one of datetime or message required. datetime: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM.

name: _BROADCAST_MSG
instruction: Broadcast a message to channels. Use when user wants to send or announce a message to Console, Telegram, or all. channels: [] = all channels; otherwise list channel names (Console, Telegram).

name: _BROWSER_VISION
instruction: Use this for ANY request to see, read, or get information from a website (including headlines, news, or summaries).

name: _LLM_SUMMARY
instruction: Use this for ANY request to summarize any text or content.
